channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: WATCH: Candidates for Las Vegas Mayor face off in a debate ahead of Election Day
item.description: Shelley Berkley will face off against Victoria Seaman in the non-partisan race. That debate will also be hosted by Denise Valdez, John Langeler, and Joshua Peguero.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, NewsNation

item.title: Why do people fill their bathtubs with water before a hurricane?
item.description: If you live in a hurricane-prone area, it may be common practice for you. If you live well outside that region, you may be confused by the move.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Hezbollah steps up rocket fire as Israel sends more troops into Lebanon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah fired another barrage of rockets into Israel on Tuesday, and the militant group’s acting leader vowed to keep up pressure that has forced tens of thousands of Israelis from their homes near the Lebanese border.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Milton's storm surge is a threat that could be devastating far beyond the Tampa Bay region
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Two weeks ago, Hurricane Helene “spared” the Tampa Bay region a direct hit and yet storm surge still caused catastrophic damage, flooding homes, drowning people who decided to stay near the coast and leaving massive piles of debris that still sit along roadsides.
item.dc:creator: BRENDAN FARRINGTON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: West Virginia woman met her husband after getting donor lungs from his late sister
item.description: A woman from West Virginia got a new chance at life and found the love of her life thanks to organ donation.
item.dc:creator: Sam Kirk, KRON4

item.title: Airline apologizes after playing explicit movie on every screen
item.description: Australia's Qantas Airways is apologizing after showing a movie that contains scenes of nudity, graphic language and sexting to the passengers aboard a recent flight from Sydney to Haneda, Japan.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: What makes a storm a hurricane? The dangers across 5 categories
item.description: The Atlantic hurricane season brings heavy rain, strong wind, storm surge and sometimes catastrophic damage to locations from the Caribbean islands and Central America, all the way up through the United States and into Canada.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dolton Mayor Tiffany Henyard walks out as trustees address budget deficit
item.description: "I just want to come here and lay the law down as it relates to what's going on in our village, where we are, and where we're going," Dolton Mayor Tiffany Henyard said.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, NewsNation

item.title: States sue TikTok, alleging platform is addictive, harms kids' mental health
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia sued TikTok on Tuesday, alleging the platform exploits and harms young users while "deceiving" the public about these dangers.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, ABC 4

item.title: U.S. Postal Service announces recommended 2024 holiday shipping dates
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service announced its recommended mailing and shipping dates on Tuesday for the 2024 holiday season. The following list shows the latest recommended dates for a package sent to an address in the contiguous United States to be delivered by Dec.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Is it safe to be outside in Hurricane Milton's eye? Here's what experts say
item.description: The eye of a hurricane is usually calm compared to the storm swirling around it — but is it safe to be outside as the eye passes over you?
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: Biden says Hurricane Milton could be 'worst storm to hit Florida in over a century'
item.description: President Biden is urging people in Florida to listen to evacuation orders as he is describing Hurricane Milton as possibly the "worst storm" to hit the state in more than a century.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Deadspin loses bid to toss defamation suit over article accusing young Chiefs fan of racism
item.description: DOVER, Del. (AP) — A Delaware judge has refused to dismiss a defamation lawsuit against sports website Deadspin over an article accusing a 9-year-old NFL fan and his family of racism because of his game-day attire. The lawsuit was filed by California residents Raul Armenta Jr.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Melania Trump waves off 'hand-swatting incident': A 'mere misunderstanding'
item.description: Melania Trump is waving off speculation that she didn't want to hold her husband's hand, chalking up images of her seemingly "swatting" away former President Donald Trump to a "mere misunderstanding.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: This Montana Senate candidate said his opponent ate 'lobbyist steak.' But he lobbied—with steak
item.description: Montana Republican Tim Sheehy denounced incumbent U.S. Sen. Jon Tester during a recent debate for “eating lobbyist steak” while Sheehy was fighting in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hurricane Milton may be October surprise in presidential battle
item.description: Strategists in both parties are looking back to Hurricane Sandy, which slammed into the mid-Atlantic states shortly before Election Day 2012.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Raccoon sparks chaos in stands during Broncos game against Raiders
item.description: Football fans packed Empower Field in Denver on Sunday to watch the Broncos take on the Las Vegas Raiders, but they weren’t the only ones in attendance.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, ABC 4

item.title: Milton again becomes Category 5 hurricane as residents flee Tampa Bay region
item.description: Fearful Florida residents streamed out of the Tampa Bay region Tuesday ahead of what could be a once-in-a-century direct hit from Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina mom of 4 swept up by Hurricane Helene flooding in front of husband, 8-year-old son
item.description: A North Carolina father of four is working to put the pieces of his life back together after flooding from Hurricane Helene took the life of his wife.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: University of Michigan president's home spray-painted with anti-Israel messages: 'Intifada'
item.description: The home of the University of Michigan president was vandalized, with anti-Israel messages spray-painted on the home on the first anniversary of Hamas' attack against Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Murdered Kansas moms: New charges filed in deaths of women on road trip
item.description: Three of the five people accused in the murder of two Kansas women earlier this year are charged with some new crimes and also had some charges dropped.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social Security COLA for 2025 predicted to be lower than 2024
item.description: The Social Security Administration will announce a new cost-of-living adjustment on Oct. 10, 2024, with the increase expected to be smaller than last year.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, Fox 5

item.title: Hawaiian fast-food worker fatally stabbed during altercation with customer: reports
item.description: Police in Honolulu, Hawaii, arrested a 60-year-old man on Sunday after he allegedly got into an argument and stabbed two Wendy's employees, one of whom later died.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A peek inside human brain shows a way it cleans out waste
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A unique peek inside the human brain may help explain how it clears away waste like the kind that can build up and lead to Alzheimer’s disease. Brain cells use a lot of nutrients which means they make a lot of waste.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: These cities are the 'safest' in America, new study says
item.description: The quality of schools, traffic and entertainment offerings are among the variety of factors you consider before moving to a new city. Another is safety.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Singer Cissy Houston, mother of Whitney Houston, dies at 91
item.description: Cissy Houston, the mother of the late Whitney Houston and a two-time Grammy winner who performed alongside superstar musicians like Elvis Presley, and Aretha Franklin, has died. She was 91.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: If you bought this ice cream, you could be eligible for a payout
item.description: If you've enjoyed vanilla-flavored Breyers ice cream over the last eight years, you may be in line for a sweet treat: a cash refund. As KTLA sister station WGN reports, Breyers is paying nearly $9 million to settle a class-action lawsuit filed against Conopco, Inc., and Unilever United States, Inc.
item.dc:creator: Alonzo Small, KTLA 5

item.title: The Titan's former lead engineer says he felt pressured to get the submersible ready
item.description: The lead engineer for an experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreck of the Titanic testified Monday that he felt pressured to get the vessel ready to dive and refused to pilot it for a journey several years earlier.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and LISA J. ADAMS WAGNER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Chicago mayor names new school board after entire panel resigns amid a fight over district control
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson named members of a new school board on Monday, batting away criticism of the move days after all seven members resigned amid an escalating fight over control of the public schools in the nation’s third-largest city.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Are hurricanes intensifying more rapidly than ever before?
item.description: Rapid intensification is defined by the National Hurricane Center as an increase of at least 30 knots (about 35 mph) in maximum sustained winds within a 24-hour period, which can mean a jump of two categories on the Saffir-Simpson scale.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Trump intends to use military to round up opponents: Michael Cohen
item.description: “The big warning that I want people to understand is when Donald Trump says something, stop sane washing it. Stop trying to make it into something which has some normalcy to it. What he says, he intends to do.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Castellanos wins Game 2 for Phillies with 9th-inning single, top Mets 7-6
item.description: Castellanos, who led the major leagues this season with four walk-off hits, tossed his helmet and was mobbed by teammates on the infield as a game that seemed to slip away one inning earlier turned into one more comeback for the NL East champions.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Stenhouse snaps 65-race losing streak after late crash at Talladega scrambles playoff picture
item.description: Ricky Stenhouse Jr. snapped a 65-race losing streak by winning in overtime at Talladega on Sunday after a late crash collected more than half the field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Here's what Spirit Halloween seeks in an 'ideal' retail location
item.description: Spirit Halloween made headlines earlier this week after firing back at "SNL" over a sketch that mocked the company's choice of retail locations.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina sets Nov. 1 execution as state ramps up use of death chamber
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina's high court on Friday set a date of Nov. 1 to put to death a man who killed a store clerk a quarter-century ago, the second of an expected six executions in about six months as the state ramps up its use of capital punishment after a 13-year pause.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4
